3.name: Tool Proficiency
3.desc: When you adopt this specialization at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with alchemist's supplies. If you already have this proficiency, you gain proficiency with one other type of artisan's tools of your choice.

3.name: Experimental Elixir
3.desc:
Whenever you finish a long rest, you can magically produce an experimental elixir in an empty flask you touch. Roll on the Experimental Elixir table for the elixir’s effect, which is triggered when someone drinks the elixir.

As an action, a creature can drink the elixir or administer it to an incapacitated creature.

You can create additional experimental elixirs by expending a spell slot of 1st level or higher for each one. When you do so, you use your action to create the elixir in an empty flask you touch, and you choose the elixir’s effect from the Experimental Elixir table.

Requirements & duration:
- You must have alchemist's supplies on your person.
- Any elixir you create lasts until it is drunk or until the end of your next long rest.

More elixirs:
- At 6th level: you can make two elixirs at the end of a long rest.
- At 15th level: you can make three elixirs at the end of a long rest.

Experimental Elixir table (d6):
- 1. Healing: The drinker regains hit points equal to 2d4 + your Intelligence modifier.
- 2. Swiftness: The drinker’s walking speed increases by 10 feet for 1 hour.
- 3. Resilience: The drinker gains a +1 bonus to AC for 10 minutes.
- 4. Boldness: The drinker can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to every attack roll and saving throw they make for the next minute.
- 5. Flight: The drinker gains a flying speed of 10 feet for 10 minutes.
- 6. Transformation: The drinker’s body is transformed as if by the Alter Self spell (drinker chooses the option); the effects last for 10 minutes.

5.name: Alchemical Savant
5.desc:
You've developed masterful command of magical chemicals.

Whenever you cast a spell using your alchemist's supplies as the spellcasting focus, you gain a bonus to one roll of the spell.

- That roll must restore hit points OR be a damage roll that deals acid, fire, necrotic, or poison damage.
- The bonus equals your Intelligence modifier (minimum of +1).

9.name: Restorative Reagents
9.desc:
You can incorporate restorative reagents into some of your works.

- Whenever a creature drinks an experimental elixir you created, the creature gains temporary hit points equal to 2d6 + your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1 temporary hit point).

- You can cast Lesser Restoration without expending a spell slot and without preparing the spell, provided you use alchemist's supplies as the spellcasting focus.
- You can do so a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

15.name: Chemical Mastery
15.desc:
You've been exposed to so many chemicals that they pose little risk to you, and you can use them to quickly end certain ailments.

- You gain resistance to acid damage and poison damage, and you are immune to the poisoned condition.

- You can cast Greater Restoration and Heal without expending a spell slot, without preparing the spell, and without providing the material component, provided you use alchemist's supplies as the spellcasting focus.
- Once you cast either spell with this feature, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest.

3.name: Tools of the Trade
3.desc: When you adopt this specialization at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with heavy armor. You also gain proficiency with smith's tools. If you already have this tool proficiency, you gain proficiency with one other type of artisan's tools of your choice.

3.name: Arcane Armor
3.desc:
As an action, you can turn a suit of armor you are wearing into Arcane Armor, provided you have smith's tools in hand.

Benefits while wearing the Arcane Armor:

- If the armor normally has a Strength requirement, it lacks that requirement for you.
- You can use the armor as a spellcasting focus for your artificer spells.
- The armor attaches to you and can’t be removed against your will. It expands to cover your entire body; you can retract or deploy the helmet as a bonus action. The armor replaces any missing limbs, functioning identically to the body part it is replacing.
- You can doff or don the armor as an action.

The armor continues to be Arcane Armor until you don another suit of armor or you die.

3.name: Armor Model
3.desc:
You can customize your Arcane Armor. Choose a model: Guardian or Infiltrator. The model grants benefits while you wear the armor.

Each model includes a special weapon. When you attack with that weapon, you can add your Intelligence modifier (instead of Strength or Dexterity) to the attack and damage rolls.

You can change the armor's model whenever you finish a short or long rest, provided you have smith's tools in hand.

Guardian:
- Thunder Gauntlets: Each gauntlet counts as a simple melee weapon while you aren't holding anything in it. On a hit, it deals 1d8 thunder damage. A creature hit by the gauntlet has disadvantage on attack rolls against targets other than you until the start of your next turn.
- Defensive Field: As a bonus action, gain temporary hit points equal to your artificer level (replacing any temp HP you already have). You lose these temp HP if you doff the armor. You can use this bonus action a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

Infiltrator:
- Lightning Launcher: A gemlike node appears on an armored fist or on the chest (your choice). It counts as a simple ranged weapon (range 90/300). On a hit, it deals 1d6 lightning damage. Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with it, you can deal an extra 1d6 lightning damage to that target.
- Powered Steps: Your walking speed increases by 5 feet.
- Dampening Field: You have advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks. If the armor normally imposes disadvantage on such checks, the advantage and disadvantage cancel each other.

5.name: Extra Attack
5.desc: Starting at 5th level, you can attack twice, rather than once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

9.name: Armor Modifications
9.desc:
You learn how to use your artificer infusions to specially modify your Arcane Armor.

- Your Arcane Armor counts as separate items for the purposes of your Infuse Items feature: armor (the chest piece), boots, helmet, and the armor's special weapon.
- Each of those items can bear one of your infusions.
- The infusions transfer over if you change your armor's model.

In addition, the maximum number of items you can infuse at once increases by 2, but those extra items must be part of your Arcane Armor.

15.name: Perfected Armor
15.desc:
Your Arcane Armor gains additional benefits based on its model.

Guardian:
When a Huge or smaller creature you can see ends its turn within 30 feet of you, you can use your reaction to force it to make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, you pull the creature up to 25 feet directly to an unoccupied space. If you pull it to within 5 feet of you, you can make a melee weapon attack against it as part of this reaction.

You can use this reaction a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

Infiltrator:
Any creature that takes lightning damage from your Lightning Launcher glimmers with magical light until the start of your next turn. The creature sheds dim light in a 5-foot radius and has disadvantage on attack rolls against you. In addition, the next attack roll against it has advantage, and if that attack hits, the target takes an extra 1d6 lightning damage.

3.name: Tool Proficiency
3.desc: When you adopt this specialization at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with woodcarver's tools. If you already have this proficiency, you gain proficiency with one other type of artisan's tools of your choice.

3.name: Eldritch Cannon
3.desc:
You've learned how to create a magical cannon.

Using woodcarver's tools or smith's tools, you can take an action to magically create a Small or Tiny eldritch cannon in an unoccupied space on a horizontal surface within 5 feet of you.

Creation limits:
- Once you create a cannon, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest OR you expend a spell slot to create one.
- You can have only one cannon at a time and can't create one while your cannon is present.

Cannon stats:
- The cannon is a magical object.
- AC 18.
- Hit points equal to 5 × your artificer level.
- Immune to poison damage and psychic damage.
- If it must make an ability check or saving throw, treat all ability scores as 10 (+0).
- If the mending spell is cast on it, it regains 2d6 hit points.

Duration & dismissal:
- It disappears if reduced to 0 hit points or after 1 hour.
- You can dismiss it early as an action.

Commanding the cannon:
- When you create it, you determine its appearance and whether it has legs.
- You choose a type from the options below.
- On each of your turns, you can take a bonus action to cause it to activate if you are within 60 feet of it.
- As part of the same bonus action, you can direct it to walk or climb up to 15 feet to an unoccupied space, provided it has legs.

Eldritch Cannon types:
- Flamethrower: The cannon exhales fire in an adjacent 15-foot cone you designate. Each creature in that area makes a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC, taking 2d8 fire damage on a failed save or half as much on a success. The fire ignites flammable objects in the area that aren't being worn or carried.
- Force Ballista: Make a ranged spell attack, originating from the cannon, at one creature or object within 120 feet of it. On a hit, the target takes 2d8 force damage, and if the target is a creature, it is pushed up to 5 feet away from the cannon.
- Protector: The cannon emits a burst of positive energy that grants itself and each creature of your choice within 10 feet of it temporary hit points equal to 1d8 + your Intelligence modifier (minimum of +1).

5.name: Arcane Firearm
5.desc:
You know how to turn a wand, staff, or rod into an arcane firearm, a conduit for your destructive spells.

When you finish a long rest, you can use woodcarver's tools to carve special sigils into a wand, staff, or rod and thereby turn it into your arcane firearm. The sigils disappear from the object if you later carve them on a different item. The sigils otherwise last indefinitely.

You can use your arcane firearm as a spellcasting focus for your artificer spells. When you cast an artificer spell through the firearm, roll 1d8, and you gain a bonus to one of the spell's damage rolls equal to the number rolled.

9.name: Explosive Cannon
9.desc:
Every eldritch cannon you create is more destructive.

- The cannon's damage rolls all increase by 1d8.
- As an action, you can command the cannon to detonate if you are within 60 feet of it. Doing so destroys the cannon and forces each creature within 20 feet of it to make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC, taking 3d8 force damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

15.name: Fortified Position
15.desc:
You’re a master at forming well-defended emplacements using Eldritch Cannon.

- You and your allies have half cover while within 10 feet of a cannon you create with Eldritch Cannon.
- You can now have two cannons at the same time. You can create two with the same action (but not the same spell slot), and you can activate both of them with the same bonus action. You determine whether the cannons are identical to each other or different. You can't create a third cannon while you have two.

actions.name: Create Eldritch Cannon
actions.desc:
Using woodcarver's tools or smith's tools, you magically create an Eldritch Cannon in an unoccupied space on a horizontal surface within 5 feet of you.

- The cannon can be Small or Tiny.
- You can create one cannon per long rest, or you can expend a spell slot to create one again.
- You can have only one cannon at a time (two at 15th level).
- The cannon lasts for 1 hour or until reduced to 0 hit points.

When you create it, choose its type: Flamethrower, Force Ballista, or Protector.

actions.name: Activate Eldritch Cannon
actions.desc:
On each of your turns, you can use a bonus action to activate your Eldritch Cannon, provided you are within 60 feet of it.

Activation effects depend on the cannon's type:

- Flamethrower: The cannon exhales fire in a 15-foot cone.
- Force Ballista: Make a ranged spell attack originating from the cannon.
- Protector: The cannon grants temporary hit points to creatures of your choice within 10 feet of it.

actions.name: Move Eldritch Cannon
actions.desc: As part of the same bonus action used to activate your Eldritch Cannon, you can direct it to walk or climb up to 15 feet to an unoccupied space, provided the cannon has legs.

actions.name: Dismiss Eldritch Cannon
actions.desc: You can use your action to dismiss your Eldritch Cannon early, causing it to disappear.

actions.name: Detonate Eldritch Cannon
actions.desc:
You command your Eldritch Cannon to detonate, provided you are within 60 feet of it.

- The cannon is destroyed.
- Each creature within 20 feet of it must make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC.
- A creature takes force damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.

3.name: Tool Proficiency
3.desc: When you adopt this specialization at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with smith's tools. If you already have this proficiency, you gain proficiency with one other type of artisan's tools of your choice.

3.name: Battle Ready
3.desc:
When you reach 3rd level, your combat training and your experiments with magic have paid off in two ways:

- You gain proficiency with martial weapons.
- When you attack with a magic weapon, you can use your Intelligence modifier, instead of Strength or Dexterity, for the attack and damage rolls.

3.name: Steel Defender
3.desc:
By 3rd level, you create a steel defender companion.

Turn & command rules:
- The defender shares your initiative count, and it takes its turn immediately after yours.
- It can move and use its reaction on its own.
- On its turn, the only action it takes is Dodge, unless you take a bonus action on your turn to command it to take another action.
- If you are incapacitated, it can take any action of its choice.

Repair & rebuilding:
- If the mending spell is cast on it, it regains 2d6 hit points.
- If it has died within the last hour, you can use smith's tools as an action to revive it (within 5 feet) by expending a spell slot of 1st level or higher. It returns to life after 1 minute with all its hit points restored.
- At the end of a long rest, you can create a new steel defender if you have smith's tools with you. If you already have one, the first one perishes.
- The defender also perishes if you die.

Steel Defender highlights:
- AC 15 (natural armor)
- HP: 2 + your Intelligence modifier + 5 × your artificer level
- Speed 40 ft.
- Immunities: poison damage; conditions: charmed, exhaustion, poisoned
- Actions: Force-Empowered Rend; Repair (3/Day)
- Reaction: Deflect Attack

5.name: Extra Attack
5.desc: Starting at 5th level, you can attack twice, rather than once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

9.name: Arcane Jolt
9.desc:
At 9th level, when either you hit a target with a magic weapon attack or your steel defender hits a target, you can channel magical energy through the strike to create one of the following effects:

- The target takes an extra 2d6 force damage.
- Choose one creature or object you can see within 30 feet of the target; it regains 2d6 hit points.

Limits:
- You can use this a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once).
- You can do so no more than once on a turn.
- You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

15.name: Improved Defender
15.desc:
At 15th level, your Arcane Jolt and steel defender become more powerful:

- Arcane Jolt extra damage/healing increases to 4d6.
- Your steel defender gains a +2 bonus to AC.
- Whenever your steel defender uses Deflect Attack, the attacker takes force damage equal to 1d4 + your Intelligence modifier.

actions.name: Command Steel Defender
actions.desc:
You command your steel defender to take an action on its turn.

- The defender normally only takes the Dodge action on its turn.
- If you take this bonus action on your turn, the defender can take another action on its turn (including one in its stat block, or another action).
- If you are incapacitated, the defender can take any action of its choice without being commanded.

actions.name: Revive Steel Defender
actions.desc:
If your steel defender has died within the last hour, you can use smith's tools as an action to begin reviving it, provided you are within 5 feet of it and you expend a spell slot of 1st level or higher.

The defender returns to life after 1 minute with all its hit points restored.

actions.name: Arcane Jolt
actions.desc:
Trigger: When you hit a target with a magic weapon attack, or when your steel defender hits a target.

Effect (choose one):
- The target takes extra force damage.
- A creature or object you can see within 30 feet of the target regains hit points.

Limits:
- Uses per long rest: Intelligence modifier (minimum 1).
- No more than once per turn.

At 15th level, the extra damage/healing increases.

3.name: Frenzy
3.desc:
Starting when you choose this path at 3rd level, you can go into a frenzy when you rage.

If you do so, for the duration of your rage you can make a single melee weapon attack as a bonus action on each of your turns after this one.

When your rage ends, you suffer one level of exhaustion.

6.name: Mindless Rage
6.desc:
Beginning at 6th level, you can't be charmed or frightened while raging.

If you are charmed or frightened when you enter your rage, the effect is suspended for the duration of the rage.

10.name: Intimidating Presence
10.desc:
Beginning at 10th level, you can use your action to frighten someone with your menacing presence.

Choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of you. If the creature can see or hear you, it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier) or be frightened of you until the end of your next turn.

On subsequent turns, you can use your action to extend the duration of this effect on the frightened creature until the end of your next turn.

This effect ends if the creature ends its turn out of line of sight or more than 60 feet away from you.

If the creature succeeds on its saving throw, you can't use this feature on that creature again for 24 hours.

14.name: Retaliation
14.desc: Starting at 14th level, when you take damage from a creature that is within 5 feet of you, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against that creature.

actions.name: Frenzy Attack
actions.desc:
Requirement: You are raging AND you chose to enter a Frenzy when you started that rage.

On each of your turns after the turn you entered your rage, you can make one melee weapon attack as a bonus action.

When your rage ends, you suffer one level of exhaustion.

actions.name: Intimidating Presence
actions.desc:
Choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of you. If the creature can see or hear you, it must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier).

On a failed save, it is frightened of you until the end of your next turn.

If the creature succeeds, you can't use this feature on that creature again for 24 hours.

actions.name: Sustain Intimidating Presence
actions.desc:
Requirement: A creature is already frightened of you from Intimidating Presence.

You use your action to extend the duration on that creature until the end of your next turn.

The effect ends if the creature ends its turn out of line of sight or more than 60 feet away from you.

actions.name: Retaliation
actions.desc:
Trigger: You take damage from a creature within 5 feet of you.

You can use your reaction to make one melee weapon attack against that creature.

3.name: Restriction: Dwarves Only
3.desc: Only dwarves can follow the Path of the Battlerager. Your DM can lift this restriction to better suit the campaign.

3.name: Battlerager Armor
3.desc:
When you choose this path at 3rd level, you gain the ability to use spiked armor as a weapon.

Spikes attack (while raging + wearing spiked armor):
- You can use a bonus action to make one melee weapon attack with your armor spikes against a target within 5 feet of you.
- On a hit, the spikes deal 1d4 piercing damage.
- You use your Strength modifier for the attack and damage rolls.

Grapple damage:
- When you use the Attack action to grapple a creature, the target takes 3 piercing damage if your grapple check succeeds.

6.name: Reckless Abandon
6.desc:
Beginning at 6th level, when you use Reckless Attack while raging, you also gain temporary hit points equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1).

These temporary hit points vanish if any of them are left when your rage ends.

10.name: Battlerager Charge
10.desc: Beginning at 10th level, you can take the Dash action as a bonus action while you are raging.

14.name: Spiked Retribution
14.desc: Starting at 14th level, when a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with a melee attack, the attacker takes 3 piercing damage if you are raging, aren't incapacitated, and are wearing spiked armor.

actions.name: Armor Spikes Attack
actions.desc:
Requirement: You are raging and wearing spiked armor.

You make one melee weapon attack with your armor spikes against a target within 5 feet of you.

- On a hit, the target takes 1d4 piercing damage.
- Use Strength for the attack and damage rolls.

actions.name: Spiked Grapple
actions.desc:
Trigger: When you use the Attack action to grapple a creature.

If your grapple check succeeds, the target takes 3 piercing damage.

actions.name: Battlerager Charge
actions.desc:
Requirement: You are raging.

You can take the Dash action as a bonus action.

actions.name: Spiked Retribution
actions.desc:
Trigger: A creature within 5 feet of you hits you with a melee attack.

If you are raging, aren't incapacitated, and are wearing spiked armor, the attacker takes 3 piercing damage.

3.name: Spirit Seeker
3.desc: At 3rd level when you adopt this path, you gain the ability to cast the Beast Sense and Speak with Animals spells, but only as rituals.

3.name: Totem Spirit
3.desc:
At 3rd level, when you adopt this path, you choose a totem spirit and gain its feature. You must make or acquire a physical totem object.

Totem options:
- Bear: While raging, you have resistance to all damage except psychic damage.
- Eagle: While raging and not wearing heavy armor, other creatures have disadvantage on opportunity attacks against you, and you can use the Dash action as a bonus action on your turn.
- Elk: While raging and not wearing heavy armor, your walking speed increases by 15 feet.
- Tiger: While raging, you can add 10 feet to your long jump distance and 3 feet to your high jump distance.
- Wolf: While raging, your friends have advantage on melee attack rolls against hostile creatures within 5 feet of you.

6.name: Aspect of the Beast
6.desc:
At 6th level, you gain a magical benefit based on a totem animal of your choice. You can choose the same animal you selected at 3rd level or a different one.

- Bear: Your carrying capacity is doubled, and you have advantage on Strength checks made to push, pull, lift, or break objects.
- Eagle: You can see up to 1 mile away with no difficulty; dim light doesn’t impose disadvantage on your Wisdom (Perception) checks.
- Elk: Your travel pace is doubled, as is the pace of up to ten companions within 60 feet of you while you're not incapacitated.
- Tiger: You gain proficiency in two skills of your choice from: Athletics, Acrobatics, Stealth, Survival.
- Wolf: You can track creatures while traveling at a fast pace, and you can move stealthily while traveling at a normal pace.

10.name: Spirit Walker
10.desc: At 10th level, you can cast Commune with Nature, but only as a ritual. When you do so, a spiritual version of one of the animals you chose for Totem Spirit or Aspect of the Beast appears to convey the information you seek.

14.name: Totemic Attunement
14.desc:
At 14th level, you gain a magical benefit based on a totem animal of your choice. You can choose the same animal you selected previously or a different one.

- Bear: While raging, hostile creatures within 5 feet of you have disadvantage on attack rolls against targets other than you or another character with this feature (immune if they can't see/hear you or if they can't be frightened).
- Eagle: While raging, you have a flying speed equal to your current walking speed, but you fall if you end your turn in the air and nothing else holds you aloft.
- Elk: While raging, you can use a bonus action during your move to pass through the space of a Large or smaller creature; it must save or be knocked prone and take 1d12 + Strength modifier bludgeoning damage.
- Tiger: While raging, if you move at least 20 feet straight toward a Large or smaller target before a melee weapon attack against it, you can use a bonus action to make one additional melee weapon attack against it.
- Wolf: While raging, you can use a bonus action on your turn to knock a Large or smaller creature prone when you hit it with a melee weapon attack.

actions.name: Dash (Totem Spirit: Eagle)
actions.desc:
Requirement: You are raging, your Totem Spirit is Eagle, and you aren't wearing heavy armor.

You can take the Dash action as a bonus action on your turn.

actions.name: Totemic Attunement: Elk Charge-Through
actions.desc:
Requirement: You are raging and your Totemic Attunement is Elk.

As a bonus action during your move, you pass through the space of a Large or smaller creature.

That creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC = 8 + your Strength modifier + your proficiency bonus) or be knocked prone and take bludgeoning damage equal to 1d12 + your Strength modifier.

actions.name: Totemic Attunement: Tiger Pounce Attack
actions.desc:
Requirement: You are raging and your Totemic Attunement is Tiger.

If you move at least 20 feet in a straight line toward a Large or smaller target right before making a melee weapon attack against it, you can use a bonus action to make one additional melee weapon attack against it.

actions.name: Totemic Attunement: Wolf Knockdown
actions.desc:
Requirement: You are raging and your Totemic Attunement is Wolf.

When you hit a Large or smaller creature with a melee weapon attack, you can use a bonus action to knock that creature prone.

3.name: Totem Spirit
3.notes: Choose one Totem Spirit at 3rd level.

6.name: Aspect of the Beast
6.notes: Choose one Aspect at 6th level. You may choose the same animal as your Totem Spirit or a different one.

6.name: Aspect of the Beast (Tiger): Skills
6.notes: Only if your Aspect of the Beast choice is Tiger.

14.name: Totemic Attunement
14.notes: Choose one Attunement at 14th level. You may choose the same animal as prior choices or a different one.

3.name: Giant’s Power
3.desc:
When you choose this path, you learn to speak, read, and write Giant or one other language of your choice if you already know Giant.

Additionally, you learn a cantrip of your choice: either druidcraft or thaumaturgy. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

3.name: Giant’s Havoc
3.desc:
Your rages pull strength from the primal might of giants, transforming you into a hulking force of destruction. While raging, you gain the following benefits:

- Crushing Throw: When you make a successful ranged attack with a thrown weapon using Strength, you can add your Rage Damage bonus to the attack’s damage roll.
- Giant Stature: Your reach increases by 5 feet, and if you are smaller than Large, you become Large, along with anything you are wearing. If there isn’t enough room for you to increase your size, your size doesn’t change.

6.name: Elemental Cleaver
6.desc:
Your bond with the elemental might of giants grows, and you learn to infuse weapons with primordial energy.

When you enter your rage, you can choose one weapon that you are holding and infuse it with one of the following damage types: acid, cold, fire, thunder, or lightning.

While you wield the infused weapon during your rage:

- The weapon’s damage type changes to the chosen type.
- It deals an extra 1d6 damage of the chosen type when it hits.
- It gains the thrown property, with a normal range of 20 feet and a long range of 60 feet.

If you throw the weapon, it reappears in your hand the instant after it hits or misses a target.

The infused weapon’s benefits are suppressed while a creature other than you wields it.

While raging and holding the infused weapon, you can use a bonus action to change the infused weapon’s current damage type to another one from the damage type options above.

10.name: Mighty Impel
10.desc:
Your connection to giant strength allows you to hurl both allies and enemies on the battlefield.

As a bonus action while raging, you can choose one Medium or smaller creature within your reach and move it to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet of yourself.

An unwilling creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier) to avoid the effect.

If, at the end of this movement, the thrown creature isn’t on a surface or liquid that can support it, the creature falls, taking damage as normal and landing prone.

14.name: Demiurgic Colossus
14.desc:
The primordial power of your rage intensifies.

When you rage:

- Your reach increases by 10 feet.
- Your size can increase to Large or Huge (your choice).
- You can use your Mighty Impel to move creatures that are Large or smaller.

In addition, the extra damage dealt by your Elemental Cleaver feature increases to 2d6.

actions.name: Change Elemental Cleaver Damage Type
actions.desc:
Requirement: You are raging and holding the weapon infused by Elemental Cleaver.

You change the infused weapon’s current damage type to another one: acid, cold, fire, thunder, or lightning.

actions.name: Mighty Impel
actions.desc:
Requirement: You are raging.

Choose one Medium or smaller creature within your reach and move it to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet of you.

An unwilling creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier) or be moved.

If it ends this movement without support, it falls as normal and lands prone.

At 14th level, you can move creatures that are Large or smaller.

3.name: Giant’s Power: Language
3.notes: You learn to speak, read, and write Giant; if you already know Giant, choose one other language instead.

3.name: Giant’s Power: Cantrip
3.notes: You learn one of these cantrips; Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for it.

3.name: Ancestral Protectors
3.desc:
Starting when you choose this path at 3rd level, spectral warriors appear when you rage.

While you are raging, the first creature you hit with an attack on your turn becomes marked by your ancestral spirits until the start of your next turn.

- The target has disadvantage on attack rolls against targets other than you.
- When the target hits a creature other than you with an attack, that creature has resistance to the damage dealt by the attack.

This effect ends early if your rage ends.

6.name: Spirit Shield
6.desc:
Beginning at 6th level, the guardian spirits that aid you can provide supernatural protection to others.

If you are raging and another creature you can see within 30 feet of you takes damage, you can use your reaction to reduce that damage by 2d6.

When you reach 10th level, this damage reduction increases to 3d6. When you reach 14th level, it increases to 4d6.

10.name: Consult the Spirits
10.desc:
At 10th level, you gain the ability to consult with your ancestral spirits.

You can cast the Augury or Clairvoyance spell without expending a spell slot or material components.

Once you cast either spell with this feature, you can't cast that spell again until you finish a short or long rest.

14.name: Vengeful Ancestors
14.desc:
At 14th level, your ancestral spirits grow powerful enough to retaliate.

When you use your Spirit Shield to reduce the damage of an attack, the attacker takes force damage equal to the amount of damage prevented by your Spirit Shield.

actions.name: Spirit Shield
actions.desc:
Trigger: While you are raging, a creature you can see within 30 feet of you takes damage.

You reduce the damage taken by 2d6.

- Damage reduction increases to 3d6 at 10th level.
- Damage reduction increases to 4d6 at 14th level.

3.name: Storm Aura
3.desc:
When you select this path at 3rd level, you emanate a stormy, magical aura while you rage. The aura extends 10 feet from you in every direction, but not through total cover.

Your aura has an effect that activates when you enter your rage, and you can activate the effect again on each of your turns as a bonus action. Choose desert, sea, or tundra. Your aura's effect depends on that chosen environment, as detailed below. You can change your environment choice whenever you gain a level in this class.

If your aura's effects require a saving throw, the DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier.

- Desert: When activated, all other creatures in your aura take 2 fire damage each. Damage increases to 3 at 5th level, 4 at 10th, 5 at 15th, and 6 at 20th.
- Sea: When activated, choose one other creature you can see in your aura. It makes a Dexterity saving throw; on a failure it takes 1d6 lightning damage, or half on a success. Damage increases to 2d6 at 10th level, 3d6 at 15th, and 4d6 at 20th.
- Tundra: When activated, each creature of your choice in your aura gains 2 temporary hit points. Temporary hit points increase to 3 at 5th level, 4 at 10th, 5 at 15th, and 6 at 20th.

6.name: Storm Soul
6.desc:
At 6th level, the storm grants you benefits even when your aura isn't active. The benefits are based on the environment you chose for your Storm Aura.

- Desert: You gain resistance to fire damage, and you don’t suffer the effects of extreme heat (DMG). Also, as an action, you can touch a flammable object that isn't being worn or carried and set it on fire.
- Sea: You gain resistance to lightning damage, you can breathe underwater, and you gain a swimming speed of 30 feet.
- Tundra: You gain resistance to cold damage, and you don’t suffer the effects of extreme cold (DMG). Also, as an action, you can touch water and turn a 5-foot cube of it into ice, which melts after 1 minute. This action fails if a creature is in the cube.

10.name: Shielding Storm
10.desc: At 10th level, you learn to use your mastery of the storm to protect others. Each creature of your choice has the damage resistance you gained from the Storm Soul feature while the creature is in your Storm Aura.

14.name: Raging Storm
14.desc:
At 14th level, the power of the storm you channel grows mightier, lashing out at your foes. The effect is based on the environment you chose for your Storm Aura.

- Desert: Immediately after a creature in your aura hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction to force it to make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, it takes fire damage equal to half your Barbarian level.
- Sea: When you hit a creature in your aura with an attack, you can use your reaction to force it to make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is knocked prone.
- Tundra: Whenever the effect of your Storm Aura is activated, you can choose one creature you can see in the aura. That creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw, or its speed is reduced to 0 until the start of your next turn.

actions.name: Storm Aura: Activate
actions.desc:
Requirement: You are raging.

You activate the effect of your Storm Aura (Desert, Sea, or Tundra). The aura also activates when you enter your rage.

Save DC (if any): 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier.

You can use this again on each of your turns as a bonus action.

actions.name: Storm Soul: Ignite Object
actions.desc:
Requirement: Your Storm Aura environment is Desert.

You touch a flammable object that isn't being worn or carried by anyone else and set it on fire.

actions.name: Storm Soul: Freeze Water
actions.desc:
Requirement: Your Storm Aura environment is Tundra.

You touch water and turn a 5-foot cube of it into ice. The ice melts after 1 minute.

This action fails if a creature is in the cube.

actions.name: Raging Storm: Desert Retaliation
actions.desc:
Requirement: You are raging AND your Storm Aura environment is Desert.

Trigger: A creature in your aura hits you with an attack.

You force that creature to make a Dexterity saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier). On a failed save, it takes fire damage equal to half your barbarian level.

actions.name: Raging Storm: Sea Knockdown
actions.desc:
Requirement: You are raging AND your Storm Aura environment is Sea.

Trigger: You hit a creature in your aura with an attack.

You force that creature to make a Strength saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier). On a failed save, the creature is knocked prone.

actions.name: Raging Storm: Tundra Freeze
actions.desc:
Requirement: You are raging AND your Storm Aura environment is Tundra.

Trigger: Whenever the effect of your Storm Aura is activated.

You can choose one creature you can see in the aura. That creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier) or its speed is reduced to 0 until the start of your next turn.

3.name: Storm Aura Environment
3.notes: Choose desert, sea, or tundra for Storm Aura. You can change this environment choice whenever you gain a level in Barbarian.

3.name: Divine Fury
3.desc:
Starting when you choose this path at 3rd level, you can channel divine fury into your weapon strikes.

While you're raging, the first creature you hit on each of your turns with a weapon attack takes extra damage equal to 1d6 + half your Barbarian level.

The extra damage is necrotic or radiant; you choose the type of damage when you gain this feature.

3.name: Warrior of the Gods
3.desc:
At 3rd level, your soul is marked for endless battle.

If a spell, such as Raise Dead, has the sole effect of restoring you to life (but not undeath), the caster doesn't need material components to cast the spell on you.

6.name: Fanatical Focus
6.desc:
Starting at 6th level, the divine power that fuels your rage can protect you.

If you fail a saving throw while raging, you can reroll it, and you must use the new roll.

You can use this ability only once per rage.

10.name: Zealous Presence
10.desc:
At 10th level, you learn to channel divine power to inspire zealotry in others.

As a bonus action, you unleash a battle cry infused with divine energy.

Up to ten other creatures of your choice within 60 feet of you that can hear you gain advantage on attack rolls and saving throws until the start of your next turn.

Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a long rest.

14.name: Rage Beyond Death
14.desc:
Beginning at 14th level, the divine power that fuels your rage allows you to shrug off fatal blows.

While you're raging, having 0 hit points doesn’t knock you unconscious.

You still must make death saving throws, and you suffer the normal effects of taking damage while at 0 hit points.

However, if you would die due to failing death saving throws, you don’t die until your rage ends, and you die then only if you still have 0 hit points.

actions.name: Divine Fury
actions.desc:
Requirement: You are raging.

Trigger: The first creature you hit on each of your turns with a weapon attack.

That creature takes extra damage equal to 1d6 + half your barbarian level.

Damage type: necrotic or radiant (chosen when you gain this feature).

actions.name: Fanatical Focus
actions.desc:
Requirement: You are raging.

Trigger: You fail a saving throw.

You reroll the save and must use the new roll.

Limit: once per rage.

actions.name: Zealous Presence
actions.desc:
Up to ten other creatures of your choice within 60 feet of you that can hear you gain advantage on attack rolls and saving throws until the start of your next turn.

Uses: once per long rest.

actions.name: Rage Beyond Death
actions.desc:
Requirement: You are raging.

While raging, having 0 hit points doesn’t knock you unconscious. You still make death saving throws and suffer the normal effects of taking damage at 0 hit points.

If you would die due to failed death saves, you don’t die until your rage ends (and then only if you still have 0 hit points).

3.name: Divine Fury Damage Type
3.notes: Choose the damage type for Divine Fury when you gain the feature.

3.name: Form of the Beast
3.desc:
Starting when you choose this path at 3rd level, when you enter your rage, you can transform, revealing the bestial power within you. Until the rage ends, you manifest a natural weapon.

- It counts as a simple melee weapon for you.
- You add your Strength modifier to the attack and damage rolls when you attack with it.

Choose the weapon’s form each time you rage:

- Bite: Deals 1d8 piercing damage on a hit. Once on each of your turns when you damage a creature with this bite, you regain hit points equal to your proficiency bonus, provided you have less than half your hit points when you hit.

- Claws: Each hand transforms into a claw (usable only if the hand is empty). Deals 1d6 slashing damage on a hit. Once on each of your turns when you attack with a claw using the Attack action, you can make one additional claw attack as part of the same action.

- Tail: Deals 1d8 piercing damage on a hit and has the reach property. If a creature you can see within 10 feet of you hits you with an attack roll, you can use your reaction to roll a d8 and add the number rolled to your AC against that attack, potentially causing it to miss.

6.name: Bestial Soul
6.desc:
Beginning at 6th level, the natural weapons of your Form of the Beast count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

When you finish a short or long rest, choose one of the following benefits, which lasts until you finish a short or long rest:

- Swimming: You gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed, and you can breathe underwater.
- Climbing: You gain a climbing speed equal to your walking speed, and you can climb difficult surfaces (including upside down on ceilings) without needing to make an ability check.
- Jumping: When you jump, you can make a Strength (Athletics) check and extend your jump by a number of feet equal to the check’s total. You can make this special check only once per turn.

10.name: Infectious Fury
10.desc:
At 10th level, when you hit a creature with your natural weapons while you are raging, the beast within you can curse your target with rabid fury.

The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC = 8 + your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus) or suffer one of the following effects (your choice):

- The target must use its reaction to make a melee attack against another creature of your choice that you can see.
- The target takes 2d12 psychic damage.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

14.name: Call the Hunt
14.desc:
At 14th level, when you enter your rage, you can choose a number of other willing creatures you can see within 30 feet of you equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of one creature).

- You gain 5 temporary hit points for each creature that accepts this feature.

Until the rage ends, each chosen creature can use the following benefit once on each of its turns:
- When the creature hits a target with an attack roll and deals damage to it, the creature can roll a d6 and gain a bonus to the damage equal to the number rolled.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

actions.name: Tail Defensive Swipe
actions.desc:
Trigger: While you are raging and have manifested the Tail, a creature you can see within 10 feet of you hits you with an attack roll.

You roll a d8 and add the number rolled to your AC against that attack, potentially causing the attack to miss.

actions.name: Infectious Fury
actions.desc:
Trigger: While you are raging, you hit a creature with your natural weapons.

The target must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC = 8 + your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus) or you choose one:

- The target uses its reaction to make a melee attack against another creature of your choice that you can see.
- The target takes 2d12 psychic damage.

Uses per long rest: proficiency bonus.

actions.name: Call the Hunt
actions.desc:
Trigger: When you enter your rage.

You can choose a number of other willing creatures you can see within 30 feet of you equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum 1).

- You gain 5 temporary hit points for each creature that accepts.
- Until the rage ends, each chosen creature can once per turn add 1d6 damage when it hits with an attack roll and deals damage.

Uses per long rest: proficiency bonus.

3.name: Totem Spirit
3.notes: Choose one Totem Spirit at 3rd level.

6.name: Aspect of the Beast
6.notes: Choose one Aspect at 6th level. You may choose the same animal as your Totem Spirit or a different one.

6.name: Aspect of the Beast (Tiger): Skills
6.notes: Only if your Aspect of the Beast choice is Tiger.

14.name: Totemic Attunement
14.notes: Choose one Attunement at 14th level. You may choose the same animal as prior choices or a different one.

3.name: Magic Awareness
3.desc:
When you choose this path at 3rd level, as an action, you can open your awareness to the presence of concentrated magic. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any spell or magic item within 60 feet of you that isn’t behind total cover. When you sense a spell, you learn which school of magic it belongs to.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

3.name: Wild Surge
3.desc:
Also at 3rd level, the magical energy roiling inside you sometimes erupts from you. When you enter your rage, roll on the Wild Magic table to determine the magical effect produced.

If the effect requires a saving throw, the DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier.

Wild Magic (d8):
1) Each creature of your choice that you can see within 30 feet of you must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take 1d12 necrotic damage. You also gain 1d12 temporary hit points.
2) You teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. Until your rage ends, you can use this effect again on each of your turns as a bonus action.
3) An intangible spirit (flumph or pixie, your choice) appears within 5 feet of one creature of your choice that you can see within 30 feet of you. At the end of the current turn, the spirit explodes, and each creature within 5 feet of it must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 1d6 force damage. Until your rage ends, you can use this effect again, summoning another spirit, on each of your turns as a bonus action.
4) Magic infuses one weapon of your choice that you are holding. Until your rage ends, the weapon's damage type changes to force, and it gains the light and thrown properties (range 20/60). If the weapon leaves your hand, it reappears in your hand at the end of the current turn.
5) Whenever a creature hits you with an attack roll before your rage ends, that creature takes 1d6 force damage.
6) Until your rage ends, you are surrounded by multicolored, protective lights; you gain a +1 bonus to AC, and while within 10 feet of you, your allies gain the same bonus.
7) Flowers and vines temporarily grow around you; until your rage ends, the ground within 15 feet of you is difficult terrain for your enemies.
8) A bolt of light shoots from your chest. Another creature of your choice that you can see within 30 feet of you must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take 1d6 radiant damage and be blinded until the start of your next turn. Until your rage ends, you can use this effect again on each of your turns as a bonus action.

6.name: Bolstering Magic
6.desc:
Beginning at 6th level, you can harness your wild magic to bolster yourself or a companion. As an action, you can touch one creature (which can be yourself) and confer one of the following benefits of your choice to that creature:

- For 10 minutes, the creature can roll a d3 whenever making an attack roll or an ability check and add the number rolled to the d20 roll.
- Roll a d3. The creature regains one expended spell slot, the level of which equals the number rolled or lower (the creature’s choice). Once a creature receives this benefit, that creature can’t receive it again until after a long rest.

You can take this action a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

10.name: Unstable Backlash
10.desc: At 10th level, immediately after you take damage or fail a saving throw while raging, you can use your reaction to roll on the Wild Magic table and immediately produce the effect rolled. This effect replaces your current Wild Magic effect.

14.name: Controlled Surge
14.desc: At 14th level, whenever you roll on the Wild Magic table, you can roll the die twice and choose which of the two effects to unleash. If you roll the same number on both dice, you can ignore the number and choose any effect on the table.

actions.name: Magic Awareness
actions.desc:
Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any spell or magic item within 60 feet of you that isn’t behind total cover. When you sense a spell, you learn which school of magic it belongs to.

Uses per long rest: proficiency bonus.

actions.name: Wild Surge: Bonus Teleport
actions.desc:
Requirement: You are raging AND your current Wild Surge effect is #2.

You teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

actions.name: Wild Surge: Summon Spirit
actions.desc:
Requirement: You are raging AND your current Wild Surge effect is #3.

You summon another intangible spirit within 5 feet of one creature you can see within 30 feet of you. At the end of the current turn, it explodes; creatures within 5 feet of it must make a Dexterity saving throw or take 1d6 force damage.

actions.name: Wild Surge: Radiant Bolt
actions.desc:
Requirement: You are raging AND your current Wild Surge effect is #8.

Another creature you can see within 30 feet of you must make a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier) or take 1d6 radiant damage and be blinded until the start of your next turn (half damage on a success; blindness only on a failed save).

actions.name: Bolstering Magic
actions.desc:
You touch one creature (which can be yourself) and choose one benefit:

- For 10 minutes, it can roll a d3 on each attack roll and ability check and add the number to the d20.
- Roll a d3. It regains one expended spell slot of level equal to the number rolled or lower (its choice). A creature can’t receive this spell-slot benefit again until after a long rest.

Uses per long rest: proficiency bonus.

actions.name: Unstable Backlash
actions.desc:
Trigger: Immediately after you take damage or fail a saving throw while raging.

You roll on the Wild Magic table and immediately produce the effect rolled. This replaces your current Wild Magic effect.

3.name: Wild Surge Table
3.notes: When you enter your rage, roll on the Wild Magic table to determine the effect.

14.name: Controlled Surge
14.notes: Whenever you roll on the Wild Magic table, you can roll twice and choose which effect to use. If both dice show the same number, you can choose any effect on the table.

3.name: Bonus Proficiencies
3.desc: You gain proficiency with three skills of your choice.

3.name: Cutting Words
3.desc: When a creature you can see within 60 feet makes an attack roll, ability check, or damage roll, you can use your reaction to expend one use of Bardic Inspiration, rolling your Bardic Inspiration die and subtracting the number rolled from the creature's roll. You can do so after the roll is made, but before the DM determines whether the attack or check succeeds or fails, or before the creature deals its damage.

6.name: Additional Magical Secrets
6.desc: You learn two spells of your choice from any class. A spell you choose must be of a level you can cast. The chosen spells count as bard spells for you, and they don't count against the number of bard spells you know.

14.name: Peerless Skill
14.desc: When you make an ability check, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration to roll your Bardic Inspiration die and add the number rolled to the check. You can choose to do so after you roll the d20, but before the DM determines whether the check succeeds or fails.

actions.name: Cutting Words
actions.desc: When a creature you can see within 60 feet of you makes an attack roll, ability check, or damage roll, you can use your reaction to expend one use of Bardic Inspiration, rolling your Bardic Inspiration die and subtracting the number rolled from the creature’s roll. You can choose to use this feature after the creature makes its roll, but before the DM determines whether the attack or ability check succeeds or fails, or before the creature deals its damage.

3.name: Guiding Whispers
3.desc: You learn the *guidance* cantrip, which doesn't count against the number of bard cantrips you know. For you, the cantrip has a range of 60 feet when you cast it.

3.name: Spiritual Focus
3.desc: You can use certain occult objects as a spellcasting focus for your bard spells: a candle, a crystal ball, a skull, a spirit board, or a similar item. Starting at 3rd level, when you cast a bard spell that deals damage or restores hit points through your Spiritual Focus, roll a d6 and gain a bonus to one damage roll or healing roll of the spell equal to the number rolled.

3.name: Tales from Beyond
3.desc: As a bonus action, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration, roll your Bardic Inspiration die, and consult the Spirit Tales table to determine the tale the spirits tell you. You retain the tale until you bestow it (or until you finish a short or long rest). As an action, you choose one creature you can see within 30 feet and bestow the tale's effect on it. You can have only one tale active at a time; rolling again replaces your current tale.

6.name: Spirit Session
6.desc: Spirits provide you with supernatural insights. As part of a short or long rest, you can conduct a 1-hour ritual with willing creatures (including yourself). At the end of the ritual, you learn one spell of your choice from any class. The spell must be of a level equal to or less than the number of creatures that participated in the ritual (including you), and it must be a divination or necromancy spell. The chosen spell counts as a bard spell for you, but it doesn't count against the number of bard spells you know. Once you perform this ritual, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

14.name: Mystical Connection
14.desc: Whenever you roll on the Spirit Tales table, you can roll the die twice and choose which effect to bestow. If you roll the same number on both dice, you can ignore the number and choose any effect on the table.

actions.name: Tales from Beyond
actions.desc: As a bonus action, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration and roll on the Spirit Tales table, retaining the tale until it is bestowed or until the start of your next long rest. You can use a bonus action to bestow the tale on one creature you can see within 30 feet of you. The tale’s effects are described in the Spirit Tales table.

actions.name: Guiding Whispers
actions.desc: You know the guidance cantrip. You can cast it without material components, and you can cast it once without expending a spell slot or requiring concentration; you regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest.

3.name: Spirit Tales Table
3.notes: When you use Tales from Beyond, roll your Bardic Inspiration die and consult the Spirit Tales table.

3.name: Bonus Proficiencies
3.desc: You gain proficiency with medium armor, shields, and martial weapons.

3.name: Combat Inspiration
3.desc: A creature that has a Bardic Inspiration die from you can add it to a weapon damage roll it just made. Alternatively, when an attack roll is made against the creature, it can use its reaction to add the Bardic Inspiration die to its AC against that attack, after seeing the roll but before knowing whether it hits or misses.

6.name: Extra Attack
6.desc: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

14.name: Battle Magic
14.desc: When you use your action to cast a bard spell, you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action.

actions.name: Battle Magic
actions.desc: When you use your action to cast a bard spell, you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action.

3.name: Mantle of Inspiration
3.desc: As a bonus action, you expend one use of Bardic Inspiration to grant yourself and a number of creatures you can see within 60 feet (up to your Charisma modifier, minimum of one) temporary hit points equal to your Bardic Inspiration die + your Charisma modifier. Each of those creatures can immediately use its reaction to move up to its speed, without provoking opportunity attacks.

3.name: Enthralling Performance
3.desc: If you perform for at least 1 minute, you can attempt to inspire awe. At the end of the performance, choose a number of humanoids within 60 feet who watched and listened (up to your Charisma modifier, minimum of one). Each target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or be charmed by you for 1 hour. While charmed this way, a creature idolizes you, speaks glowingly of you to anyone who talks to it, and hinders anyone who opposes you, though it avoids violence unless it was already inclined to fight on your behalf. This effect ends early if the target takes damage, if you attack it, or if it witnesses you attacking or damaging any of its allies.

6.name: Mantle of Majesty
6.desc: As a bonus action, you cast *command* without expending a spell slot, and you take on an appearance of otherworldly beauty for 1 minute. While this feature is active, you can cast *command* as a bonus action on each of your turns, without expending a spell slot. Any creature charmed by you automatically fails its saving throw against the *command* you cast with this feature. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

14.name: Unbreakable Majesty
14.desc: As a bonus action, you assume a magically majestic presence for 1 minute. Whenever a creature tries to attack you for the first time on its turn, it must make a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, it can't attack you on that turn, and it must choose a new target for its attack or the attack is wasted. On a successful save, it can attack you normally, and it is immune to this feature for 24 hours. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

actions.name: Mantle of Inspiration
actions.desc: As a bonus action, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration to grant yourself a wondrous appearance. When you do so, choose a number of creatures you can see within 60 feet of you, up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one). Each creature gains temporary hit points equal to your Bardic Inspiration die + your Charisma modifier and can immediately use its reaction to move up to its speed, without provoking opportunity attacks.

actions.name: Mantle of Majesty
actions.desc: As a bonus action, you can cast command without expending a spell slot. For 1 minute, you can cast command in this way as a bonus action on each of your turns. Any creature charmed by you automatically fails its saving throw against the command you cast with this feature.

actions.name: Unbreakable Majesty
actions.desc: When a creature attacks you for the first time on a turn, you can use your reaction to attempt to turn the attack away. The creature must make a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature must choose a new target for the attack or the attack automatically misses.

3.name: Bonus Proficiencies
3.desc: You gain proficiency with medium armor and the scimitar.

3.name: Fighting Style
3.desc: You adopt a fighting style: **Dueling** (when wielding a melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon) or **Two-Weapon Fighting** (when you engage in two-weapon fighting, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of the second attack).

3.name: Blade Flourish
3.desc: Whenever you take the Attack action on your turn, your walking speed increases by 10 feet until the end of the turn. Also, if a weapon attack you make as part of that action hits a creature, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration to deal extra damage equal to the number you roll on the Bardic Inspiration die and apply one of the following effects: **Defensive Flourish** (add the Bardic Inspiration die to your damage roll, and increase your AC by the number rolled until the start of your next turn); **Slashing Flourish** (deal the Bardic Inspiration die as damage to one other creature of your choice you can see within 5 feet of the original target); **Mobile Flourish** (push the target up to 5 feet away from you, plus a number of feet equal to the number rolled on the Bardic Inspiration die, and you can immediately use your reaction to move up to your walking speed to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of it).

6.name: Extra Attack
6.desc: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

14.name: Master's Flourish
14.desc: Whenever you use a Blade Flourish, you can roll a d6 and use it instead of expending a Bardic Inspiration die.

actions.name: Blade Flourish
actions.desc: When you take the Attack action on your turn, your walking speed increases by 10 feet until the end of the turn, and if you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration to perform one of the following flourishes: Defensive Flourish, Slashing Flourish, or Mobile Flourish. You roll your Bardic Inspiration die and apply the result according to the chosen flourish.

3.name: Psychic Blades
3.desc: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration to deal an extra 2d6 psychic damage to that target. You can use this feature once per turn. The psychic damage increases as you gain levels in this class: 3d6 at 5th level, 5d6 at 10th level, and 8d6 at 15th level.

3.name: Words of Terror
3.desc: If you speak to a humanoid alone for at least 1 minute, you can attempt to seed paranoia and fear. At the end of the conversation, the creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or be frightened of you (or another creature you choose) for 1 hour. If the target succeeds, it has no hint that you tried to frighten it. Once a target fails its saving throw, it is frightened in this way for the full duration and can't repeat the save. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

6.name: Mantle of Whispers
6.desc: When a humanoid dies within 30 feet of you, you can use your reaction to capture its shadow. You retain this shadow until you use it or until you finish a long rest. As an action, you can don the shadow, magically transforming to look like the dead creature for 1 hour. While you wear the shadow, you gain access to its general memories (such as recognizing its allies and enemies and its general habits), and you can pass yourself off as that person by mimicking its speech and mannerisms. The disguise ends early if you dismiss it (no action required) or if you are incapacitated.

14.name: Shadow Lore
14.desc: As an action, you magically whisper a phrase that only one creature of your choice within 30 feet can hear. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or be charmed by you for the next 8 hours or until you or your companions attack it, damage it, or force it to make a saving throw. The charmed creature obeys your commands and does its best to fulfill your requests. If you or your companions do anything harmful to it, the effect ends. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

actions.name: Psychic Blades
actions.desc: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration to deal an extra 2d6 psychic damage to that target. The damage increases as you gain bard levels.

actions.name: Mantle of Whispers
actions.desc: When a humanoid dies within 30 feet of you, you can use your reaction to magically capture its shadow. As an action, you can use the captured shadow to assume the appearance of the humanoid for 1 hour, gaining access to information the humanoid would freely share with an ally. The disguise ends early if you are attacked or cast a spell.

3.name: Mote of Potential
3.desc: Whenever you give a creature a Bardic Inspiration die, you can also create a Tiny mote of potential that orbits within 5 feet of that creature. The mote is intangible and invulnerable, and it lasts until the Bardic Inspiration die is lost. When the creature uses the Bardic Inspiration die, the mote provides an additional effect based on how the die was used: **Ability Check** (the creature can roll the Bardic Inspiration die twice and use either result); **Attack Roll** (immediately after the creature rolls the Bardic Inspiration die to add it to an attack roll, the mote thunderously shatters; the target and each creature of your choice that you can see within 5 feet of it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC or take thunder damage equal to the number rolled on the Bardic Inspiration die); **Saving Throw** (immediately after the creature rolls the Bardic Inspiration die to add it to a saving throw, the mote vanishes with a soothing musical note; the creature gains temporary hit points equal to the number rolled on the Bardic Inspiration die + your Charisma modifier, minimum of 1 temporary hit point).

3.name: Performance of Creation
3.desc: As an action, you can create one nonmagical item of your choice in an unoccupied space within 10 feet of you. The item must appear on a table of items in the equipment chapter of the Player's Handbook. It must be Medium or smaller and worth no more than 20 gp × your bard level, and the item must be of a form and material that you have seen before. The created item disappears after a number of hours equal to your proficiency bonus. Once you create an item with this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend a spell slot of 2nd level or higher to use this feature again. You can have only one item created by this feature at a time; if you use this action and already have an item from this feature, the first one immediately vanishes.

6.name: Animating Performance
6.desc: As an action, you can animate one Large or smaller nonmagical item within 30 feet of you that isn't being worn or carried. The animated item uses the Dancing Item stat block and is friendly to you and your companions. In combat, it shares your initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It can move and use its reaction on its own, but the only action it takes on its turn is the Dodge action, unless you use a bonus action on your turn to command it to take another action. The item remains animated for 1 hour, until it is reduced to 0 hit points, or until you die. Once you animate an item with this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend a spell slot of 3rd level or higher to use this feature again.

14.name: Creative Crescendo
14.desc: When you use Performance of Creation, you can create more than one item at once. The number of items equals your Charisma modifier (minimum of one). If you create an item and you already have one or more items from this feature, you can choose which previously created items (if any) immediately vanish. In addition, you are no longer limited by gp value when creating items with Performance of Creation, and you can create items of Huge size or smaller.

actions.name: Performance of Creation
actions.desc: As an action, you can create one nonmagical item in an unoccupied space within 10 feet of you. The item must appear on a surface or in a liquid that can support it. The gp value of the item can't be more than 20 × your bard level, and the item must be Medium or smaller (or an object that fits within a 5-foot cube). The item is visibly magical (e.g., it glimmers) and lasts for a number of hours equal to your proficiency bonus, until you use this feature again, or until it takes any damage.

actions.name: Animating Performance
actions.desc: As an action, you can target one Large or smaller nonmagical item within 30 feet of you and animate it as a Dancing Item for 1 hour. The Dancing Item uses the stat block from the feature, and it is friendly to you and your companions. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus per long rest (or expend a spell slot of 3rd level or higher to use it again).

actions.name: Command Dancing Item
actions.desc: On your turn, you can use a bonus action to command your Dancing Item (if you have one) to take an action in its stat block or to move. If you don't issue a command, it takes the Dodge action and uses its movement to avoid danger.

3.name: Silver Tongue
3.desc: When you make a Charisma (Persuasion) or Charisma (Deception) check, you can treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.

3.name: Unsettling Words
3.desc: As a bonus action, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration and choose one creature you can see within 60 feet. Roll your Bardic Inspiration die. The next time before the start of your next turn that the creature makes a saving throw, it subtracts the number rolled from the save.

6.name: Unfailing Inspiration
6.desc: When a creature adds one of your Bardic Inspiration dice to its ability check, attack roll, or saving throw and the roll still fails, the creature can keep the Bardic Inspiration die.

6.name: Universal Speech
6.desc: As an action, choose a number of creatures within 60 feet of you up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one). For 1 hour, the chosen creatures can magically understand you, regardless of the language you speak. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend a spell slot to use it again.

14.name: Infectious Inspiration
14.desc: When you successfully give a creature a Bardic Inspiration die, you can use your reaction to inspire another creature (other than yourself) that you can see within 60 feet, without expending another use of Bardic Inspiration. You can do this a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

actions.name: Unsettling Words
actions.desc: As a bonus action, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration and choose one creature you can see within 60 feet of you. Roll your Bardic Inspiration die; the creature subtracts the number rolled from the next saving throw it makes before the start of your next turn.

actions.name: Universal Speech
actions.desc: As an action, you can choose a number of creatures within 60 feet of you equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one). For 1 hour, the chosen creatures can understand your speech, regardless of the language you speak. This feature ends early if you are incapacitated or if you use it again.

actions.name: Infectious Inspiration
actions.desc: When a creature within 60 feet of you adds one of your Bardic Inspiration dice to an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw and the roll succeeds, you can use your reaction to encourage a different creature (other than yourself) within 60 feet of you, giving it a Bardic Inspiration die without expending a use of Bardic Inspiration.

1.name: Arcane Initiate
1.desc: When you choose this domain at 1st level, you gain proficiency in the Arcana skill, and you gain two cantrips of your choice from the wizard spell list. For you, these cantrips count as cleric cantrips.

2.name: Channel Divinity: Arcane Abjuration
2.desc:
Starting at 2nd level, you can use your Channel Divinity to abjure otherworldly creatures.

As an action, you present your holy symbol, and one celestial, elemental, fey, or fiend of your choice within 30 feet of you must make a Wisdom saving throw, provided the creature can see or hear you. On a failed save, the creature is turned for 1 minute or until it takes any damage.

A turned creature must spend its turns trying to move as far away from you as it can, and it can't willingly end its move in a space within 30 feet of you. It also can't take reactions. For its action, it can only use the Dash action or try to escape from an effect that prevents it from moving. If there's nowhere to move, it can use the Dodge action.

After you reach 5th level, when a creature fails its saving throw against this feature, the creature is banished for 1 minute (as in the banishment spell, no concentration required) if it isn't on its plane of origin and its challenge rating is at or below the following threshold: 5th level (CR 1/2 or lower), 8th (CR 1 or lower), 11th (CR 2 or lower), 14th (CR 3 or lower), 17th (CR 4 or lower).

6.name: Spell Breaker
6.desc: Starting at 6th level, when you restore hit points to an ally with a spell of 1st level or higher, you can also end one spell of your choice on that creature. The level of the spell you end must be equal to or lower than the level of the spell slot you use to cast the healing spell.

8.name: Potent Spellcasting
8.desc: Starting at 8th level, you add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

17.name: Arcane Mastery
17.desc: At 17th level, you choose four spells from the wizard spell list, one from each of the following levels: 6th, 7th, 8th, and 9th. You add them to your list of domain spells. Like your other domain spells, they are always prepared and count as cleric spells for you.

actions.name: Channel Divinity: Arcane Abjuration
actions.desc: As an action, you present your holy symbol, and one celestial, elemental, fey, or fiend of your choice within 30 feet of you that can see or hear you must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is turned for 1 minute or until it takes any damage. Starting at 5th level, if the creature isn’t on its plane of origin and its CR is at or below your Arcane Banishment threshold, it is banished for 1 minute instead (as the banishment spell, no concentration required).

1.name: Arcane Initiate: Wizard Cantrips
1.notes: Choose two cantrips from the wizard spell list. For you, these cantrips count as cleric cantrips.

17.name: Arcane Mastery: Wizard Spells
17.notes: Choose four spells from the wizard spell list: one 6th-, one 7th-, one 8th-, and one 9th-level spell. They become domain spells for you (always prepared; count as cleric spells).

1.name: Bonus Proficiency
1.desc: When you choose this domain at 1st level, you gain proficiency with martial weapons.

1.name: Reaper
1.desc: At 1st level, you learn one necromancy cantrip of your choice from any spell list. When you cast a necromancy cantrip that normally targets only one creature, the spell can instead target two creatures within range and within 5 feet of each other.

2.name: Channel Divinity: Touch of Death
2.desc: Starting at 2nd level, you can use Channel Divinity to destroy another creature's life force by touch. When you hit a creature with a melee attack, you can use Channel Divinity to deal extra necrotic damage to the target. The damage equals 5 + twice your cleric level.

6.name: Inescapable Destruction
6.desc: Starting at 6th level, necrotic damage dealt by your cleric spells and Channel Divinity options ignores resistance to necrotic damage.

8.name: Divine Strike
8.desc: At 8th level, once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 necrotic damage to the target. When you reach 14th level, the extra damage increases to 2d8.

14.name: Divine Strike Improvement
14.desc: Starting at 14th level, the extra necrotic damage of your Divine Strike increases to 2d8.

17.name: Improved Reaper
17.desc: Starting at 17th level, when you cast a necromancy spell of 1st through 5th level that targets only one creature, the spell can instead target two creatures within range and within 5 feet of each other. If the spell consumes its material components, you must provide them for each target.

actions.name: Channel Divinity: Touch of Death
actions.source: Death Domain 2 (Channel Divinity: Touch of Death)
actions.desc: When you hit a creature with a melee attack, you can use Channel Divinity to deal extra necrotic damage to the target. The extra damage equals 5 + twice your cleric level.

1.name: Reaper: Necromancy Cantrip
1.notes: Choose one necromancy cantrip from any class spell list. It counts as a cleric cantrip for you.

1.name: Blessings of Knowledge
1.desc: At 1st level, you learn two languages of your choice. You also become proficient in two skills of your choice from the following list: Arcana, History, Nature, or Religion. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of those skills.

2.name: Channel Divinity: Knowledge of the Ages
2.desc: Starting at 2nd level, you can use Channel Divinity to gain proficiency in any skill or tool. As an action, you gain proficiency in one skill or tool of your choice for 10 minutes.

6.name: Channel Divinity: Read Thoughts
6.desc: At 6th level, you can use Channel Divinity to read a creature’s thoughts. As an action, you choose one creature you can see within 60 feet of you. For 1 minute, you can read its surface thoughts (as if concentrating on the detect thoughts spell). During that time, you can use your action to end the effect early and cast suggestion on the creature without expending a spell slot. The target automatically fails its saving throw against the suggestion spell.

8.name: Potent Spellcasting
8.desc: Starting at 8th level, you add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

17.name: Visions of the Past
17.desc: At 17th level, you can call up visions of the past that relate to an object you hold or your immediate surroundings. You spend at least 1 minute in meditation and prayer, then receive dreamlike, shadowy glimpses of recent events. This feature can be used once per short or long rest.

actions.name: Channel Divinity: Knowledge of the Ages
actions.source: Knowledge Domain 2 (Channel Divinity: Knowledge of the Ages)
actions.desc: As an action, you use your Channel Divinity to gain proficiency in one skill or tool of your choice for 10 minutes.

actions.name: Channel Divinity: Read Thoughts
